message.content: I just logged out and signed up as a new user and still had the data from the previous user. Make sure on logout we clear all react queries, delete all local data so like no state is still there after logout.

content.thinking: The user wants to ensure that when a user logs out, all React Query cache data and any other local state is cleared. Let me first understand the current logout implementation and how React Query is set up in the web app.

content.text: Let me look at the current logout implementation and React Query setup.

## Analysis: Web App Auth, Logout, Query Client, and Client-Side State

### Overview

The web app (`apps/web`) uses `better-auth/react` for authentication, `@tanstack/react-query` for server state management, and `@orpc/tanstack-query` for typed RPC calls. Logout is triggered from two UI locations, both calling `authClient.signOut()` from the better-auth client. Client-side state persistence is done via `localStorage` for date range and filter selections, and via `next-themes` for the theme preference.

---

### 1. Logout Implementation

There are exactly two places where logout is triggered in the web app.

#### Sidebar Logout Button (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx:224-232`)

```tsx
<button
    type="button"
    onClick={() => authClient.signOut()}
    className="p-1 rounded-md text-muted hover:text-foreground hover:bg-hover transition-colors shrink-0"
    title="Sign out"
>
    <LogOut className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
</button>
```

This button is rendered in the bottom section of the sidebar at line 224, only when the sidebar is not collapsed (line 224: `{!collapsed && (`). It calls `authClient.signOut()` without chaining any `.then()` callback. The `authClient` is imported from `../../lib/auth-client` at line 21.

After `signOut()` completes, better-auth internally invalidates its session cache. The `authClient.useSession()` hook at `App.tsx:39` will then return `data: null`, causing the `App` component to re-render and show the login/signup form instead of the dashboard routes (the `if (!session)` check at `App.tsx:72`).

#### Profile Page Logout Button (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx:66-77`)

```tsx
<Button
    variant="outline"
    size="sm"
    onClick={() => {
        authClient.signOut().then(() => {
            window.location.reload();
        });
    }}
>
    <LogOut className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
    Sign out
</Button>
```

This button calls `authClient.signOut()` and explicitly chains `.then(() => { window.location.reload(); })` at line 70-72. This forces a full page reload after sign-out completes, which clears all in-memory React state in addition to the server-side session invalidation.

#### What `authClient.signOut()` does

`authClient` is created via `createAuthClient` from `better-auth/react` at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts:4-7`. The `signOut()` method is provided by the better-auth library. It sends a POST request to the better-auth sign-out endpoint on the server (routed through the Vite proxy at `/api` -> `http://localhost:4010`, as configured in `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/vite.config.ts:11`). The server invalidates the session, and the better-auth client internally clears its cached session data, causing `useSession()` to return `null`.

#### Post-Logout Behavior

- **Sidebar logout**: No explicit page reload. The `useSession()` hook in `App.tsx:39` reactively returns `null`, and the `App` component renders the login/signup UI at lines 72-81 instead of the `<Routes>` tree.
- **Profile page logout**: Explicit `window.location.reload()` at line 71, which destroys all in-memory state and reinitializes the app from scratch.

Neither logout path clears `localStorage` items (`dateRange`, `globalFilters`). Those values persist across logout/login cycles.

---

### 2. React Query / TanStack Query Client Setup

#### QueryClient Creation (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/main.tsx:1-9`)

```tsx
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query";
// ...
const queryClient = new QueryClient();
```

The `QueryClient` is instantiated at module scope on line 9 with no custom configuration (default options for `staleTime`, `gcTime`, `retry`, etc.).

#### QueryClientProvider (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/main.tsx:12-22`)

```tsx
createRoot(document.getElementById("root")!).render(
    <StrictMode>
        <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
            <BrowserRouter>
                <ThemeProvider>
                    <App />
                </ThemeProvider>
            </BrowserRouter>
        </QueryClientProvider>
    </StrictMode>,
);
```

`QueryClientProvider` wraps the entire application tree at line 14. The provider hierarchy from outermost to innermost is: `StrictMode` -> `QueryClientProvider` -> `BrowserRouter` -> `ThemeProvider` -> `App`.

#### QueryClient Usage in OrganizationContext (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx:1,34,50,59`)

The `OrganizationProvider` accesses the `queryClient` via `useQueryClient()` at line 34. It calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries()` (with no filter arguments, meaning it invalidates all queries) in two places:

- Line 50: After auto-setting the active org when the user has orgs but none is active.
- Line 59: After the user manually switches orgs via `switchOrg()`.

#### ORPC TanStack Query Integration (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/lib/orpc.ts:4,15`)

```tsx
import { createTanstackQueryUtils } from "@orpc/tanstack-query";
// ...
export const orpc = createTanstackQueryUtils(client);
```

The `orpc` object at line 15 wraps the typed ORPC client and provides TanStack Query utilities (query keys, query functions) that integrate with the `QueryClient`. The ORPC client itself is created at lines 7-13 with `credentials: "include"` on every fetch, ensuring cookies are sent with RPC requests.

---

### 3. Auth Context and Auth Hooks

#### Auth Client (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`)

```tsx
import { organizationClient } from "better-auth/client/plugins";
import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/react";

export const authClient = createAuthClient({
    baseURL: "",
    plugins: [organizationClient()],
});
```

The `authClient` is the single auth singleton for the web app. Key details:

- `baseURL: ""` at line 5 means all auth API requests use relative URLs (e.g., `/api/auth/...`), which the Vite dev server proxies to `http://localhost:4010`.
- The `organizationClient()` plugin at line 6 adds organization-related methods and hooks: `authClient.organization.setActive()`, `authClient.organization.acceptInvitation()`, `authClient.organization.rejectInvitation()`, `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`.

#### Auth Hooks in Use

The `authClient` object provides React hooks from `better-auth/react`. These are used directly (there are no custom wrapper hooks for auth):

| Hook | Used In | File:Line |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `authClient.useSession()` | `App` component | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/App.tsx:39` |
| `authClient.useSession()` | `Sidebar` component | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx:138` |
| `authClient.useSession()` | `ProfilePage` component | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx:19` |
| `authClient.useSession()` | `AcceptInvitationPage` component | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx:10` |
| `authClient.useActiveOrganization()` | `OrganizationProvider` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx:31` |
| `authClient.useListOrganizations()` | `OrganizationProvider` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx:33` |

Note: `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/hooks/useSession.ts` exports a `useSession` function, but this is unrelated to auth -- it is a hook for parsing Claude Code session conversation data (line 19: `export function useSession(session: Session)` where `Session` is a session transcript type, not an auth session).

#### Auth Methods Called Imperatively

| Method | Used In | File:Line |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `authClient.signIn.email()` | `LoginForm` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/login-form.tsx:39` |
| `authClient.signIn.social()` (Google) | `LoginForm` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/login-form.tsx:93` |
| `authClient.signIn.social()` (GitHub) | `LoginForm` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/login-form.tsx:104` |
| `authClient.signUp.email()` | `SignupForm` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/signup-form.tsx:29` |
| `authClient.signOut()` | `Sidebar` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx:227` |
| `authClient.signOut()` | `ProfilePage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx:70` |
| `authClient.listAccounts()` | `ProfilePage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx:26` |
| `authClient.linkSocial()` | `ProfilePage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx:129` |
| `authClient.organization.setActive()` | `OrganizationProvider` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx:49` |
| `authClient.organization.setActive()` | `OrganizationProvider` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx:58` |
| `authClient.organization.setActive()` | `AcceptInvitationPage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx:44` |
| `authClient.organization.acceptInvitation()` | `AcceptInvitationPage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx:33` |
| `authClient.organization.rejectInvitation()` | `AcceptInvitationPage` | `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx:55` |

---

### 4. Local Storage Usage and Client-Side State Persistence

There are three categories of `localStorage` usage in the web app.

#### Date Range Persistence (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/DateRangeContext.tsx`)

- **Key**: `"dateRange"` (line 22: `const STORAGE_KEY = "dateRange"`)
- **Read**: On initialization at lines 41-51, `getInitialDates()` checks URL search params (`from`/`to`) first, then falls back to `localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)`, then falls back to a default 7-day range.
- **Write**: On every date change at lines 74-78, the effect at lines 71-91 writes `JSON.stringify({ start: startDate, end: endDate })` to localStorage.
- **Format**: `{"start":"2026-02-17","end":"2026-02-24"}` (ISO date strings)

#### Filter Persistence (`/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/FilterContext.tsx`)

- **Key**: `"globalFilters"` (line 21: `const STORAGE_KEY = "globalFilters"`)
- **Read**: On initialization at lines 34-45, `getInitialFilters()` checks URL search params (`devs`/`repos`) first, then falls back to `localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY)`, then falls back to empty arrays.
- **Write**: On every filter change at lines 75-83, the effect at lines 72-110 writes `JSON.stringify({ developers: [...], repositories: [...] })` to localStorage.
- **Format**: `{"developers":["user1","user2"],"repositories":["repo1"]}`

#### Theme Persistence (via `next-themes`)

- `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/providers/ThemeProvider.tsx:3-8` configures `next-themes` with `attribute="class"` and `defaultTheme="light"`.
- `next-themes` internally uses `localStorage` with the key `"theme"` (the library's default) to persist the user's light/dark/system preference.

#### No Auth Token Storage

The auth client (`better-auth/react`) manages sessions via HTTP-only cookies, not via `localStorage` or `sessionStorage`. The `credentials: "include"` setting on the ORPC fetch at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/lib/orpc.ts:9` ensures cookies are sent with RPC requests. There is no explicit auth token storage in the web app's client-side code.

---

### 5. Auth Client Setup (`better-auth/react`)

#### File: `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`

```tsx
import { organizationClient } from "better-auth/client/plugins";
import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/react";

export const authClient = createAuthClient({
    baseURL: "",
    plugins: [organizationClient()],
});
```

This is the complete file -- 7 lines total.

- **`createAuthClient`** (from `better-auth/react`): Creates a client that provides both imperative methods (like `signIn.email()`, `signOut()`) and React hooks (like `useSession()`). The React variant (vs. `better-auth/client`) adds hook-based reactivity.
- **`baseURL: ""`**: Empty string means all requests go to the same origin. In dev, the Vite proxy at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/vite.config.ts:10-13` forwards `/api` requests to `http://localhost:4010`.
- **`organizationClient()` plugin**: Adds the `authClient.organization.*` methods and the `useActiveOrganization()` / `useListOrganizations()` hooks.

#### Where `authClient` is Imported

The `authClient` is imported in 8 files total:

| File | Import Line |
|------|-------------|
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/App.tsx` | Line 6 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` | Line 21 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/login-form.tsx` | Line 2 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/components/auth/signup-form.tsx` | Line 2 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx` | Line 9 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx` | Line 5 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/ProfilePage.tsx` | Line 6 |
| `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/OrganizationPage.tsx` | (via import) |

---

### Data Flow: Logout Sequence

**Sidebar logout path:**

1. User clicks the logout icon button in `Sidebar.tsx:224-232`
2. `authClient.signOut()` is called at line 227
3. better-auth sends a POST to the server's sign-out endpoint (cookie-based)
4. Server invalidates the session in Postgres
5. better-auth client internally clears its session cache
6. `authClient.useSession()` in `App.tsx:39` reactively returns `{ data: null }`
7. `App` component re-renders, the `if (!session)` branch at line 72 kicks in
8. Login/signup form is rendered instead of the dashboard routes

**Profile page logout path:**

1. User clicks "Sign out" button in `ProfilePage.tsx:66-77`
2. `authClient.signOut()` is called at line 70
3. Same server-side invalidation as above
4. `.then(() => { window.location.reload(); })` fires at line 71
5. Full page reload destroys all React state, re-fetches `index.html`
6. `main.tsx` re-runs, creates fresh `QueryClient`, mounts `App`
7. `authClient.useSession()` returns `null` (session was invalidated)
8. Login form is shown

### Key Pattern: Session-Gated Routing

The `App` component at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/App.tsx:38-111` acts as the auth gate. The `authClient.useSession()` hook at line 39 drives all routing decisions:

- `isPending === true` (line 56): Shows "Loading..." spinner
- `session === null` (line 72): Shows login/signup form
- `session !== null` (line 84): Renders the full `<Routes>` tree with `DashboardLayout`

There is no separate auth context provider or auth guard component. The `authClient.useSession()` hook itself is the reactive auth state, and `App` is the single point where authenticated vs. unauthenticated UI is switched.

### Data Flow: Logout Sequence

**Sidebar logout path:**

1. User clicks the logout icon button in `Sidebar.tsx:224-232`
2. `authClient.signOut()` is called at line 227
3. better-auth sends a POST to the server's sign-out endpoint (cookie-based)
4. Server invalidates the session in Postgres
5. better-auth client internally clears its session cache
6. `authClient.useSession()` in `App.tsx:39` reactively returns `{ data: null }`
7. `App` component re-renders, the `if (!session)` branch at line 72 kicks in
8. Login/signup form is rendered instead of the dashboard routes

**Profile page logout path:**

1. User clicks "Sign out" button in `ProfilePage.tsx:66-77`
2. `authClient.signOut()` is called at line 70
3. Same server-side invalidation as above
4. `.then(() => { window.location.reload(); })` fires at line 71
5. Full page reload destroys all React state, re-fetches `index.html`
6. `main.tsx` re-runs, creates fresh `QueryClient`, mounts `App`
7. `authClient.useSession()` returns `null` (session was invalidated)
8. Login form is shown

### Key Pattern: Session-Gated Routing

The `App` component at `/Users/marc/Workspace/rudel/apps/web/src/App.tsx:38-111` acts as the auth gate. The `authClient.useSession()` hook at line 39 drives all routing decisions:

- `isPending === true` (line 56): Shows "Loading..." spinner
- `session === null` (line 72): Shows login/signup form
- `session !== null` (line 84): Renders the full `<Routes>` tree with `DashboardLayout`

There is no separate auth context provider or auth guard component. The `authClient.useSession()` hook itself is the reactive auth state, and `App` is the single point where authenticated vs. unauthenticated UI is switched.
